summary: The redesigned Orbit featuring a higher aspect ratio for improved upwind performance and even more lift. A significant step forward from the 2025 version with refined aerodynamics.

sources.summary: A meaningful redesign over the 2025 model. The higher AR brings better upwind and more lift.
Noticeable improvement over the previous Orbit. Better low-end and more predictable looping behavior.
The 2026 redesign puts the Orbit firmly in competition with the Core XR and Duotone Rebel for the big air crown.

Thrive 9M and my 8 m Evo dlab another factor that I find arguably more important than the bar pressure itself is how that pressure relates to the speed of the kite what I would call the balance in relation to that pressure you apply this was something I couldn't really get the hang of so much with the core pace for example as it was not particularly heavy on the bar just riding around but as you went to move the kite you felt a lot more power that was hard to translate into good control and it did make it take more effort than most of the other Lula kites I've ridden the other her as I mentioned in that review was that the kite was lightning fast so I would constantly overdo a lot of my movements because I was putting so much weight into my movements through the bar and the kite would react so quickly in the Pace's defense I had also just reviewed the Harlem Thrive which by all accounts is one of the lightest bars pressures on the market with whatever you do and the kite is excellent balanced with its lightning fast speed why I mention all of this is that one thing I find I like most about the orbits in general is just how balanced the pressure is in relation to the speed of the kites this balance makes them all fairly easy for me to just hop on and use in such a range of conditions however as I did say with the orbit Ultra it does get more challenging as the so when it comes to testing other kites they don't tend to really send it as aggressively or consistently when the wind is stronger which into their defense and mine is not the easiest thing to do on Loops then the ultra would be my pick in those sizes as I mentioned for me I still think the pro is fine in the 10 and upsize and and is probably going to work for most Riders even more progressing Riders as it seems to be just the right balance of speed and ease of use for froths looking to keep on looping as their kites get a little bit larger so as always if you like this review please like The 2026 Orbit has a narrower arc as well as a even higher aspect ratio than the previous one making it more efficient in the sky while having less drag cuz uh it's more to the side of the wind window. structured_review.summary: The 2026 North Orbit is a meaningful redesign rather than an incremental update, featuring a higher aspect ratio, thinner leading edge, swept-back wingtips, new NRS3 canopy, and the MX2 airframe previously reserved for the Orbit Pro — all at the standard Orbit price. It sits further forward in the window and handles overpowered conditions better than its predecessor. The core Orbit DNA remains: accessible, user-friendly big air with balanced bar pressure, though it still trails the top dedicated big-air kites in raw lift and hangtime.
pros: MX2 airframe (previously Pro-only) at standard Orbit price
Higher aspect ratio improves upwind efficiency and reduces drag
Kite sits further forward in window, aiding jump initiation
New NRS3 triple ripstop canopy replaces D2 material
More relaxed, controlled feel in overpowered conditions
Five-point bridle maintains shape on slimmer leading edge
cons: Still a tier below top big-air kites in raw lift and hangtime
Increased steering effort before kite accelerates (vs 2025)
Not versatile — weak low end, poor fit for waves or foiling
Real-world performance data limited; beach test only so far for 2026
structured_review.best_for: Intermediate to advanced riders focused on big air and kite loops who want a user-friendly, durable kite with genuine performance upgrades at a competitive price.
structured_review.not_for: Riders who need a versatile all-rounder for foiling, waves, or light-wind sessions, or those chasing maximum raw lift above all else.
structured_review.rec_blurb: Orbit Pro DNA at Orbit Classic prices — the biggest redesign in the model's history.
